etymology.description: semantic 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 良(良) (liáng) -> wave
etymology.interpretation: The character 浪(làng) ("wave") combines the semantic 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") with the phonetic 良(良) (liáng) to suggest the sound and concept of waves in water. The use of 良(良) provides the phonetic element while 水(水) directly contributes the meaning, linking the character to the concept of waves on a body of water.

examples.chinese: 海上的浪很大(海上的浪很大)。
examples.pinyin: Hǎi shàng de làng hěn dà.
examples.english: The waves on the sea are very big.

examples.chinese: 我们在那里欣赏海浪(我們在那裡欣賞海浪)。
examples.english: We are there to admire the waves.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢在海边冲浪(他喜歡在海邊沖浪)。
examples.english: He enjoys surfing at the beach.
